title: Configuring Java Call-In
text: Configuring Java Call-In Uniface uses the Java Runtime Environment (JRE) to support Java call-in at runtime, and the Java Developer Kit for compiling and packaging generated Java wrapper files. You need to configure Uniface so that it can locate these facilities when required, and you need to enable Java to find the Uniface Java call-in DLLs. A Java Developer Kit must be installed. During installation, you can choose to install the JRE provided with Uniface (OpenJDK 17), or you can have Uniface use an existing JRE installation. By default, the installer configures Uniface to use the jvm.dll provided by the specified JRE by adding the location to the [USER_3GL] of the usys.asn file. Configure Uniface for Java call-in: Edit the Windows environment variables PATH and CLASSPATH: PATH—add the paths to the JDK /bin directory and the Uniface common/bin directory. CLASSPATH—add the path to the ujci30.jar file in the Uniface common/bin directory. Ensure that the location of the jvm.dll is specified in the [USER_3GL] section of the usys.asn file. Note: The JRE is also used for other Uniface functionality, so if you change this value, you may also need to perform other steps. Configure the JDK to locate the Uniface call-in DLLs by setting the system property uniface.bindir. For example: java.lang.System.setProperty("uniface.bindir","C://Uniface 10//common//bin"); Copy or java -Duniface.bindir="C://Uniface 10//common//bin" Copy Note: For other Uniface call-in properties, you can use the ConfigManager or ConfigManagerBean .
